candidates.why: Sales and marketing expenses are an operating cost, not top-line revenue.
Financial income is non-operating and not total revenue from operations.
Interest income is a finance item, not operating revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the period
candidates.why: Comprehensive income includes net income plus OCI, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Contract Liabilities - Short-Term - Advance Consideration & Progress Billings in Excess of Unbilled Revenue
candidates.why: A balance sheet liability, not a revenue line.

final_choice.row_label: Total revenue and other operating income

variables.notes: None of the provided candidates represent total revenue. The appropriate top-line is the income statement row 'Total revenue and other operating income', which aggregates revenue and other operating income. This row was selected from the full A-column outside the candidate list, so manual review is required. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Total revenue and other operating income

candidates.why: Explicitly labeled as cost of goods sold; most direct mapping for COGS.
These are operating personnel costs, generally part of operating expenses/SG&A rather than direct COGS in this presentation.
A broad operating expense bucket, likely includes SG&A, not just direct cost of sales.
Selling and marketing are SG&A-type costs, not direct COGS.
Overhead lease costs, part of operating expenses/SG&A.
Legal and litigation are overhead/SG&A, not COGS.
Residual operating expenses, mixed overhead; not specifically COGS.
Total operating expenses (sum of components), not just direct COGS. Components exist, so this total should be avoided.
Financing costs, not cost of sales.
Other financial costs, not COGS.
Net financing result, not production/service costs.
R&D expense is research-related, usually separate from COGS.
Balance sheet liability; not an income statement COGS line.

variables.notes: 'Cost of Goods Sold' is an explicit COGS line and is preferred over broader operating expense buckets or totals.

candidates.why: This is income, not an operating expense; should not be classified as SG&A.
A classic overhead operating expense bucket; fits SG&A components.
Total of operating expense components; since detailed components exist, this total should be avoided for SG&A mapping.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles - Supplemental
candidates.why: This is amortization (a D&A item), which should be excluded from SG&A according to the instructions.

variables.notes: No single SG&A line exists, but multiple operating overhead components are disclosed. Following the hierarchy for XSGA_COMPONENTS, detailed operating expense components are selected and totals (e.g., 'Operating expenses') and D&A lines are excluded. Selected rows are classic SG&A-type overhead: personnel, other operating, sales & marketing, lease, legal/litigation, bad debt, and residual operating expense. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Employee benefit expenses, [Income Statement] Sales and marketing expenses, [Income Statement] Short-term lease expenses, [Income Statement] Legal Provisions & Litigation Expenses, [Income Statement] Bad Debt Written-Off/Provision, [Income Statement] Other Operating Expense - Remaining

candidates.why: Amortization of previously capitalized R&D, not the current-period R&D expense itself.
Explicitly labeled current-period R&D expense; best match for XRD.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: Also amortization of capitalized R&D, not the R&D expense line.
Balance sheet asset for capitalized R&D, not an expense.
Accumulated amortization on capitalized R&D, not a period expense line item.

variables.notes: 'Research & Development Expense - Supplemental' is the clearest current-period R&D expense line and is preferred over amortization or balance sheet capitalization lines.

candidates.why: Direct interest expense line; primary choice for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest component of lease liabilities; relevant interest expense if main line is missing for some years.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease-related interest; can serve as backup label.
Interest income (opposite sign) and should not be used while explicit expense lines exist.
Balancing value associated with interest expense; good fallback if primary interest lines are missing in some years.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Multiple interest expense-related rows exist. They are ordered as a priority list so later processing can take the first non-missing value per year, preferring the general interest expense line, then lease-related interest, then balancing/supplemental values.

candidates.row_label: Equity holders of the parent company
candidates.why: This is an income attribution line (net income to equity holders), not a balance sheet equity amount.
Component of equity but not clearly the full equity attributable to owners; a more direct total equity line exists.
Subcomponent/adjustment within equity; not total owner equity.
Direct total equity line; in absence of a specific 'attributable to owners' line, this is the best proxy for book equity.
Duplicate labeling/capitalization of 'Total equity'. Either represents the same total; only one is needed to avoid duplication.

variables.notes: No separate line for 'Equity attributable to owners of the parent' exists. 'Total equity' is therefore used as book equity, and MIB is set to zero by leaving it empty so as not to double count.
No balance sheet line for non-controlling interests or minority interest is present. MIB is therefore set to an empty list, implying zero minority interest in the PROF computation.
